name: wind_farm_bat_migration_lease_energy
premise: Energy commissioner Sofia Anders must decide whether to grant a curtailed-operation variance to RidgeLine Wind during peak bat migration weeks. Ornithologist Dr. Ben Cole shows acoustic deterrence reduced fatalities by half last season but cut output enough to threaten winter heating reserves for two rural counties. Environmental groups promise injunctions if variance is denied; grid operators warn of brownouts if turbines spin unrestricted. Lease payments fund local schools; losing the variance may push the developer to sell to fossil backup investors.

agents.name: Sofia Anders
agents.identity: I am the state energy commissioner responsible for grid reliability commitments and statutory wildlife protection mandates.
agents.goal: I want clean power scaled without signing off on predictable ecological harm that undermines public trust in renewables.

agents.name: Dr. Ben Cole
agents.identity: I am a conservation biologist who monitors bat migration corridors and turbine curtailment effectiveness with acoustic and thermal sensors.
agents.goal: I want curtailment rules enforced during peak migration nights even if that reduces megawatt hours during cold snaps.

choices: I will deny the variance and enforce full curtailment during migration peaks, accepting higher brownout risk and political backlash from fuel-poor counties.
I will grant the variance with mandatory acoustic deterrence and post-season mortality audits, accepting residual bat losses above strict conservationists' targets.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The governor wants a signed order before the evening news cycle. Sofia Anders must choose between strict curtailment or a variance with deterrence conditions. What does Sofia Anders do?
